survivors_flagged.one_line: Array sub running serial spectrum special divs ($11/sh paid Jun-25-26) + TDS all-stock buy-in of the ~18% Array stub with a $10.40/sh pre-close dividend; live dated capital-return/structural play (asymmetry sits mostly in AD).
Anglo American merger-of-equals: shareholder + Canada approved Dec 2025, but final close pending China/Korea, expected Sep-2026-to-Mar-2027 — live dated arb spread, not yet closed.
Olanzapine LAI (TEV-749) FDA decision/launch targeted Q4 2026 (NDA accepted Feb-2026) plus duvakitug readouts; real dated approval but a pharma-pipeline bet more than a structural mispricing.
Daseke/truckload (incl. specialized TL) spin-off slated 2026-27, but explicitly contingent on landing another LTL carrier and 'potentially delayed' — real but soft/un-hardened.
Board-approved tax-free separation into two public cos (RemainCo vascular/interventional + NewCo urology/acute/OEM) targeted mid-2026 — dated, forward, structural.
Wholesale-fiber/infra spin into TIF subsidiary = capital-unlock thesis (BofA upgraded on it), but dissent-tender window already ran (Dec-2025) and spin largely disclosed/priced.
Dated PDUFA Sep-11-2026 (Pixclara glioma) plus Zircaix kidney resubmission — real dated binaries, but routine radiopharma pipeline approvals, not a mispriced special-situation asymmetry.

NOAA full-compliance reached May-2026, application now in certification stage on a dated path to a potential final US seabed-mining permit by early 2027 — live regulatory binary on a critical-minerals supply-shortage moat.
Definitive Berkshire Hathaway all-cash takeover at $72.50/sh announced May-31-2026, expected close 2H-2026 — clean dated merger-arb on a remaining spread / customary-conditions risk.
Pending US AD/CVD circumvention probe (Ethiopia duty-evasion claim) is a dated regulatory binary, but it's a downside overhang on a 4GW solar ramp rather than a clean forward upside catalyst.
Telesat GEO ~$2.4B debt maturing Dec-2026 onward + 90%-creditor fraudulent-transfer suit; dated restructuring/LME wall but equity is most-subordinated claim.
Live contested merger arb — special meeting 7/2/2026 (imminent); CrossCountry $12 cash vs UWM $12.50, 54% voting no, genuine bid-bump asymmetry.
Live merger arb — Blackstone $61.25 cash, shareholder + PUCT + FERC approvals DONE, closes 2H26; tight definitive-deal spread (px ~57 vs 61.25).
Textron announced 4/30/2026 separation of Industrial segment (spin or sale), 12-18mo timeline — real forward structural catalyst but distant/early.

verified.catalyst: TDS's non-binding all-stock buy-in of the ~18% Array (AD) public stub (0.86 TDS/AD) assumes a $10.40/share (~$900M) pre-close special dividend, with the special-committee outcome and remaining unsold C-band spectrum proceeds the still-unpriced legs.
verified.dated_milestone: Array special committee (PJT/Cravath, formed May 8 2026) still evaluating the May 7 2026 TDS proposal; next gate = committee recommendation / definitive merger agreement, expected H2-2026 (no fixed date disclosed).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty TDS) + G2 (concrete commitment: 0.86 exchange ratio assuming a $10.40/sh pre-close dividend) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: ~$900M pre-close div + unmonetized C-band spectrum value not yet in the offer)
verified.edge: M — structure is named, dated and forward, but TDS already controls 81.9% / 95.9% of votes, so this is a controlled-shareholder squeeze-out: minority leverage to push the 0.86 ratio higher is weak and the proposal remains non-binding; asymmetry is real but capped.
verified.primary_source: TDS Form 8-K / offer letter exhibit (SEC, filed May 8 2026, dated May 7 2026); Array AD 8-K forming special committee (May 8 2026); Array $11 div PR (Jun 11/25 2026 dates).
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs SEC 8-Ks (via StockTitan filing quotes): TDS proposal dated May 7 2026 (announced May 8), 0.86 TDS/AD all-stock, explicitly NON-BINDING, conditioned on Array paying a $10.40/sh (~$900M) pre-close special dividend; special committee formed May 8 2026 with PJT Partners + Cravath; TDS holds 81.9% capital / 95.9% voting (line's ~82%/96% accurate). The forward leg is genuinely live and unpriced. CORRECTIONS: (1) the $11.00/sh dividend (record Jun 11, paid Jun 25 2026, off $1.0B Verizon + $168M T-Mobile = $1.168B proceeds) is per the filing UNRELATED to the TDS proposal and already FIRED — so the $10.40 pre-close div is a SEPARATE, additional capital-return on top of the $11, not the same instrument; the line's FIRED-vs-forward distinction is correct. (2) The 'remaining unsold C-band spectrum as largest unmonetized asset' claim could NOT be confirmed in primary filings (they say only 'opportunistically monetize remaining spectrum' with no C-band inventory) — asserted, not sourced. Controlled-shareholder cap on minority squeeze-out upside is real; correctly sized as a capital-return/event play, not deep asymmetry.
verified.analysis: The $11/sh special div (paid Jun 25 2026 off the $1.0B Verizon + $168M T-Mobile spectrum sales) is already FIRED and cannot be the thesis. The live, forward leg is the May 7 2026 TDS proposal to take in the AD public stub at 0.86 TDS shares, explicitly conditioned on Array first paying a $10.40/sh (~$900M) pre-close dividend once the announced spectrum sales close — a dated, specific, currently-unpriced capital-return structure now under independent-committee review (formed May 8 2026, PJT Partners + Cravath), with AD's remaining C-band the largest unmonetized asset. Risk is the controlled-shareholder dynamic (TDS holds ~82%/96% of votes) which caps minority squeeze-out upside and keeps the bid non-binding, so size it as a capital-return/event play, not a deep-asymmetry bet.

verified.catalyst: Anglo American / Teck all-share merger of equals (1.3301 AAL shares per TECK share) awaits its final regulatory gate — China antitrust — to close, with a live ~12% gross spread to deal terms still on the table.
verified.dated_milestone: China antitrust clearance (sole remaining approval); final approval/close guided Sep 2026 to Mar 2027 — next watch: Q3-2026 window opens September 2026
verified.instrument: Long TECK vs short 1.3301 AAL.L per share (ratio merger-arb); or long TECK outright if expressing copper-champion upside
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Anglo American plc), G2 (concrete commitment: signed plan of arrangement, fixed 1.3301 exchange ratio, both shareholder bases + Canada + South Korea already approved), G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: ~12% gross spread of TECK $58.19 vs implied ~$65 at 1.3301 x AAL.L £36.27)
verified.edge: M — spread is wide (~12% gross) precisely because of real residual China-approval risk plus a long, uncertain close window (as late as Mar 2027 ≈ 9mo carry), and the TECK leg floats with AAL's GBP share price, so an un-hedged long carries copper/FX/market beta rather than pure deal alpha.
verified.primary_source: mining.com / Anglo American spokesperson, "between September 2026 and March 2027" (article dated 2026-03-18); MLex "only China remains" (Q2-2026); deal terms per teck.com merger release 2025-09-09; live quotes TECK $58.19 / AAL.L 3627 GBp (FMP, 2026-06-29)
verified.kill_fact: Core thesis holds against primary sources: the Anglo American/Teck all-share merger of equals (announced 9 Sep 2025, fixed 1.3301 AAL per TECK) cleared both shareholder votes (9 Dec 2025, >99% Anglo) and the Investment Canada Act, with China antitrust the key outstanding regulatory gate and management reiterating a verbatim Sep-2026-to-Mar-2027 final-approval window — a dated, forward, not-yet-fired event. The special US$4.19/sh dividend correctly accrues to Anglo holders pre-close, not TECK (CONFIRMED). TWO CORRECTIONS: (1) Magnitude overstated — at live quotes TECK $58.19 vs AAL.L 3627p (£36.27) and GBP/USD 1.3253, implied USD value per TECK = 1.3301 x £36.27 x 1.3253 = ~$63.94, a gross spread of ~9.9%, NOT ~$65 / ~12%; the triage implicitly used GBP/USD ~1.345. (2) 'China the SOLE outstanding gate' is not cleanly confirmed — MLex says S. Korea cleared (Q2-2026, China only), but mining.com quotes an Anglo spokesperson still anticipating clearance from BOTH China AND South Korea. The ~10% spread is not clean cash-merge edge: it carries Anglo GBP equity + copper + FX beta plus China deal-break risk.
verified.analysis: Triage is verified and accurate: the Anglo/Teck merger of equals (announced Sep 2025) has cleared both shareholder votes, the Investment Canada Act, and South Korea (Q2-2026), leaving China antitrust as the sole outstanding regulatory condition, with the company reiterating a Sep-2026-to-Mar-2027 final-approval window — a dated, forward, not-yet-fired event. At the fixed 1.3301 ratio, TECK at $58.19 trades roughly 12% below the implied ~$65 value of the AAL.L stock consideration (£36.27), a concrete unpriced figure that survives the strict gate. Risk is idiosyncratic (China clearance + timeline) rather than macro, though it is not a clean cash-merge: the consideration is AAL stock, so realized value drifts with Anglo's GBP share price and copper, and the special US$4.19 dividend accrues to Anglo holders pre-close, not to TECK.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on TEV-749 (once-monthly subcutaneous olanzapine LAI for schizophrenia), with NDA accepted Feb 20, 2026 and a standard 10-month review pointing to a late-2026 (~Q4) approval/launch decision.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA decision ~Q4 2026 (late 2026; ~Dec 2026, 10 months from Feb 20, 2026 NDA acceptance)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 weak (concrete regulatory milestone — NDA accepted, PDUFA pending — but a generic launch, not a contracted commercial commitment); G3 weak (only figure is a combined olanzapine+Uzedy >$2B peak-sales analyst estimate, broadly known/priced, not a TEV-749-specific currently-unpriced number); G1 fail (no named counterparty contract).
verified.edge: L — real dated FDA decision but a single-asset pharma-pipeline binary, not a structural mispricing; Uzedy/olanzapine optionality is well-flagged by sell-side and stock has already re-rated (now $33.31 vs $14.99 52-wk low, above $29.11 200-day).
verified.primary_source: Teva/GlobeNewswire press release, "FDA Accepts Teva's NDA for Olanzapine ER Injectable Suspension (TEV-'749)," Feb 20, 2026; PDUFA late-2026 per standard 10-month review.
verified.analysis: TEV-749's late-2026 PDUFA decision is a genuine, dated, forward, un-fired regulatory catalyst (NDA accepted Feb 20, 2026; ~10-month review), and the Phase 3 SOLARIS data showing no post-injection monitoring requirement is a differentiator versus existing olanzapine LAIs. However, this is the pharma-pipeline bet the triage flagged rather than a structural mispricing: there is no named-counterparty commercial contract (G1), the launch is generic (G2 weak), and the only quantified upside is a combined olanzapine+Uzedy >$2B peak-sales estimate that is already broadly priced into a stock that has more than doubled off its 52-week low. Duvakitug adds optionality but only has Phase 2b maintenance data with Phase 3 underway and no dated 2026 readout, so it is not a near-term catalyst — net WATCH, score 5.

verified.catalyst: Planned spin-off of TFI's Truckload/Daseke (specialized TL) division into a separately traded company, but explicitly gated on first closing a ~$4-5B US LTL acquisition for scale.
verified.dated_milestone: No hardened date; management guides "2026-2027 and potentially delayed." Next update marker = Q2 2026 earnings call (~late July 2026), a reporting cadence not a commitment.
verified.bloom_gates: None met. G1 fail: no named counterparty (LTL target unidentified). G2 fail: no concrete commitment — no Form 10, no board-approved separation date, language is "potentially delayed." G3 fail: company has published no specific currently-unpriced SoP/spin valuation figure.
verified.edge: L — narrative is widely known since the April 2024 Daseke deal and reiterated each call; entirely contingent on a macro-dependent ($4-5B LTL M&A in a soft freight cycle) event with no firm date, so no asymmetric unpriced edge.
verified.primary_source: Trucking Dive, "TFI will delay spinoff of Daseke, other truckload assets" (Q3 2024 call, ~Oct 2024); reaffirmed contingent/soft on TFII Q1 2026 6-K earnings (StockTitan, Apr 2026).
verified.analysis: The Daseke/Truckload spin-off is real as a stated management intention but remains soft and un-hardened: it is explicitly conditional on first landing a ~$4-5B US LTL acquisition that has no identified counterparty, and TFI itself calls the 2026-27 timing "potentially delayed." On the Q1 2026 call (revenue $1.95B, LTL operating income -35%, "very early signs" of TL improvement) Bedard reaffirmed acquisitions before a split rather than committing to a separation date or filing. This is a contingent, macro/M&A-dependent conditional plan, not a dated forward catalyst — it fails all three Bloom gates, so tier NONE / score 3.

verified.catalyst: The "tax-free separation into two public companies" thesis is stale/dead: Teleflex pivoted to selling its acute care, interventional urology and OEM units for cash (~$2.03B gross / ~$1.8B after-tax), not a Form 10 spin-off NewCo.
verified.dated_milestone: Two cash divestitures (OEM to Montagu/Kohlberg $1.5B; Acute Care + Interventional Urology to Intersurgical $530M) expected to close 2H 2026; OEM deal has a termination backstop of Sep 1, 2026 (Dec 1, 2026 with extension). Next dated check: 2H-2026 close.
verified.bloom_gates: none cleanly — the flagged catalyst (tax-free 2-way public separation) no longer exists; the replacement is a signed-but-already-disclosed cash sale (would be G1 named buyers Montagu/Kohlberg + Intersurgical with G3 figures $1.5B/$530M), but it is already public and largely re-rated, so it fails the FORWARD/unpriced test for the flagged thesis.
verified.edge: L — the structural spin-off the flag describes was abandoned; the cash-sale replacement was announced and is widely covered (signed deals, fixed prices, 2H-2026 close), so it is disclosed/priced rather than an unpriced forward asymmetry.
verified.primary_source: Teleflex "Reports 2025 Financial Results and Full Year 2026 Outlook," investors.teleflex.com (Feb 26, 2026); corroborated by MedTech Dive "Teleflex inks deals to sell 3 businesses for $2B" (2026).
verified.analysis: Teleflex originally (Feb 2025) intended a tax-free spin of NewCo (urology/acute/OEM) vs RemainCo (vascular/interventional/surgical), but per the Feb 26, 2026 results release and MedTech Dive coverage it switched to OUTRIGHT SALES after inbound buyer interest — OEM to Montagu/Kohlberg for $1.5B and Acute Care + Interventional Urology to Intersurgical for $530M, ~$1.8B after-tax to fund buybacks/debt paydown. The flagged "separation into two public cos targeted mid-2026" milestone is therefore stale and no longer the live structure; what remains is a signed, publicly-disclosed cash divestiture with a 2H-2026 close, not an unpriced forward spin-off optionality. Gate STRICT: dead/changed catalyst plus a re-rated replacement → tier NONE, score 3.

verified.catalyst: Telkom is carving its wholesale fiber into 99.9%-owned InfraNexia (PT Telkom Infrastruktur Indonesia/TIF) and aims to sell a 20-30% strategic-partner stake at 9-12x EV/EBITDA, unlocking ~Rp11-25T of proceeds plus 4-9% incremental dividend yield.
verified.dated_milestone: Phase 2 full fiber-asset transfer (IDR 90T/$5.4B) targeted H2 2026; strategic-partner stake sale has NO firm date — CEO (Jun 19 2026) says "being considered," BRIDS warns Icon+ consolidation may push partner entry to 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: Rp11-25T equity proceeds + 4-9% incremental dividend yield from a 20-30% stake at 9-12x EV/EBITDA, BRIDS Apr-2026). G1 FAILS — no named counterparty. G2 weak — no signed commitment, only "under consideration."
verified.primary_source: BRI Danareksa Sekuritas (BRIDS) note "TLKM ... Potential Icon+ Consolidation to Reignite Infranexia's Value Unlocking Story," 9 Apr 2026 (TP Rp4,000, FV range Rp4,100-4,500); CNN Indonesia 19 Jun 2026 (CEO: strategic partner not IPO, no date); FMP quote TLK $13.67 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The thesis is real but softer on timing than the headline implies, and the triage's "largely priced" claim is wrong on direction: the TLK ADR sits at its 52-week low ($13.67 vs $19.23 200-day avg), so the market is NOT crediting any value unlock — the asymmetry is intact (G3 met: a specific, currently-unpriced Rp11-25T proceeds figure plus 4-9% incremental dividend yield from a 20-30% InfraNexia stake at 9-12x EV/EBITDA). What it lacks is a fired or datable catalyst: Phase 1 (the Dec-2025 spin decree) already ran, Phase 2 is a 99.9%-owned internal reorg (not a cash event), and the actual value-unlock leg — an external strategic partner — has no named counterparty (G1 fails), no signed commitment (G2 weak: CEO said "under consideration" on Jun 19 2026), and BRIDS warns the Icon+/PLN consolidation may push partner entry into 2027. Net: genuine SoP discount and a credible forward path, but the binding catalyst is undated and slip-prone, so WATCH/5 — re-rate to ACTIVE only if a named partner or a firm stake-sale timeline (or NeutraDC monetization) is announced.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on Pixclara (TLX101-Px / 18F-FET) PET imaging agent for recurrent/progressive glioma, a dated diagnostic-approval binary.
verified.dated_milestone: Pixclara PDUFA goal date Sep-11-2026 (NDA resubmitted Mar-13-2026, FDA acceptance Apr-9-2026); Zircaix/TLX250-CDx has NO dated milestone — CRL on Aug-28-2025, still un-resubmitted as of Dec-22-2025 with only a Jan-2026 FDA data-plan meeting.
verified.instrument: equity (NASDAQ: TLX ADR, also ASX: TLX)
verified.bloom_gates: None cleanly met. No named counterparty (G1), no concrete unpriced commitment (G2), and no specific currently-unpriced figure (G3) — the Pixclara approval is well-telegraphed (orphan + fast-track, 18F-FET already in NCCN guidelines), so the date is real but the asymmetry is consensus, not mispriced.
verified.edge: L — a dated FDA binary exists, but it is a routine diagnostic-imaging orphan approval at a non-distressed valuation ($11.30, ~$3.8B cap, mid-range vs 52w 6.28-16.98, near 50/200d avgs); revenue contribution is modest and the outcome is broadly expected, so there is no unpriced asymmetry. Net overhang from an active Hagens Berman securities class action (prostate-drug misstatements + supply chain) further argues against an upside-skewed special-sit.
verified.primary_source: Telix/GlobeNewswire "FDA Accepts NDA for TLX101-Px (Pixclara)" PDUFA Sep-11-2026 (Apr-9-2026); Telix portfolio update on TLX250-CDx CRL remediation (Dec-22-2025); FMP quote TLX $11.30 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: Pixclara's Sep-11-2026 PDUFA is a genuine dated, forward, un-fired binary, so the name survives as WATCH — but it is exactly the routine radiopharma diagnostic approval the triage flagged, not a mispriced special situation: the agent is orphan/fast-track and 18F-FET is already guideline-recommended internationally, making approval the consensus base case rather than an unpriced figure. The second leg, Zircaix/TLX250-CDx, fails the dated test entirely — after the Aug-2025 CRL it remains un-resubmitted with no new PDUFA date as of the Dec-22-2025 update (only a January FDA data-plan meeting), so it is an open-ended CMC remediation, not a forward binary. No Bloom gate clears and valuation is mid-range/non-distressed amid a live securities class action, so this is WATCH (score 5), not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: NOAA's review of TMC USA's consolidated deep-seabed mining application — in full compliance since May 1, 2026 and now in the certification stage — is on a dated path toward a final US commercial-recovery permit decision targeted before the end of Q1 2027, the first-ever such permit.
verified.dated_milestone: Certification stage / Federal Register posting now (USA B exploration license certified May 28, 2026); next gating steps = draft EIS → public comment → final EIS; final commercial-recovery permit decision targeted by end of Q1 2027 (March 31, 2027).
verified.instrument: Equity (long); single-name regulatory binary, sizeable optionality given $4.23 vs $11.35 year-high — LEAP calls expressing the Q1 2027 decision window are the cleaner risk-defined vehicle.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: NOAA, the US permitting authority) + G2 (concrete dated commitment: full-compliance reached May-2026, certification stage entered, company-guided final permit decision by end Q1 2027)
verified.edge: M — the path and dates are public and the supply-shortage thesis is well-covered, but the tape has NOT priced a win: stock sits at $4.23, near year-low $3.93 and far below 50/200-day MAs ($5.37/$6.23), so a favorable certification/EIS sequence or an early permit signal is asymmetric vs a tape treating approval as uncertain. Edge capped at M (not H) because outcome timing and EIS/litigation risk are genuine and the binary is widely watched.
verified.primary_source: TMC GlobeNewswire press release "NOAA Determines TMC USA's Consolidated Deep-Seabed Mining Application is in Full Compliance" (May 1, 2026); corroborated by NOAA USA B exploration license certification (May 28, 2026) and FMP quote $4.23 (2026-06-29).
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY-CONFIRMED: NOAA's May 1, 2026 GlobeNewswire/SEC release states TMC USA's consolidated deep-seabed mining application (exploration license + commercial recovery permit, ~65,000 km2 CCZ) is in FULL COMPLIANCE and now moves into the certification stage, on a milestone path the company expects to 'result in a permit before the end of Q1 2027' — the first-ever such US commercial-recovery permit. USA-B exploration license separately CERTIFIED May 28, 2026 (primary GlobeNewswire). Live quote confirms un-fired/not-re-rated: $4.23 vs yearHigh $11.35, yearLow $3.93. Forward dated regulatory binary stands. Minor correction: the line slightly conflates the consolidated commercial-recovery track (Q1-2027 target) with the distinct USA-B exploration-only certification; the multi-step EIS path (NOI->draft EIS->public comment->final EIS) is the real binary risk and the company's Q1-2027 date is a guidance target, not a NOAA-committed statutory deadline.
verified.analysis: Verified via TMC's primary GlobeNewswire release and NOAA filings: the application reached full compliance May 1, 2026 and moved into the certification stage (USA B exploration license certified May 28, 2026), on a company-guided path to a final commercial-recovery permit decision before end of Q1 2027 — a genuine, dated, forward regulatory binary on a critical-minerals (nickel/cobalt/copper/manganese) supply-shortage moat, not a macro bet. The remaining gating steps (draft EIS, public comment, final EIS) inject real binary risk and the company's own Q1-2027 target is the load-bearing date; an EIS slip or adverse environmental/legal challenge is the primary downside. Score 7 / ACTIVE: real named counterparty (G1) and a concrete dated commitment (G2), un-fired and not re-rated (price near year-lows), but held below 8 because the catalyst is widely known and the multi-step EIS path leaves the precise decision date and outcome uncertain.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Berkshire Hathaway all-cash takeover of Taylor Morrison at $72.50/share (~$6.8B equity / ~$8.5B EV), DEFM14A filed, board unanimous, special meeting July 22 2026, expected close 2H-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote at virtual special meeting July 22, 2026; HSR/antitrust clearance pending; expected close 2H-2026.
verified.instrument: equity (long common for residual cash spread)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Berkshire Hathaway, definitive agreement); G2 (concrete $72.50/sh all-cash commitment, signed merger agreement). G3 fails — the deal price is essentially fully priced.
verified.edge: L — the 24% announcement pop is already captured; live $71.78 vs $72.50 leaves only a ~1.0% gross spread (~2.5-4% annualized to 2H-2026 close), the canonical thin Berkshire-arb. Forward edge is residual carry + customary-conditions/timing risk, not an unpriced re-rate.
verified.primary_source: Taylor Morrison DEFM14A merger proxy (StockTitan/SEC) + Yahoo Finance "Berkshire Hathaway to acquire Taylor Morrison for $72.50 per share in cash," announced May 31, 2026; live FMP quote $71.78 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: Catalyst is fully real and dated: Berkshire Hathaway's $72.50/share all-cash acquisition of Taylor Morrison was announced May 31, 2026, DEFM14A is filed with a unanimous board, the shareholder vote is set for July 22, 2026, and management guides to a 2H-2026 close pending HSR. However, the catalyst is effectively FIRED — the stock re-rated 24% on announcement and now trades at $71.78, just ~1.0% below the $72.50 cash price, so the only remaining return is a sub-low-single-digit arb spread plus modest annualized carry. Clean dated merger-arb, but the unpriced upside is gone; appropriate as a low-risk WATCH-tier spread, not an ACTIVE high-conviction catalyst.

verified.catalyst: US Commerce anti-circumvention inquiry into Ethiopian solar (petition names TOYO's Hawassa 4GW cell plant) — a regulatory binary that is a downside duty overhang on the Ethiopia-to-US route, not a forward upside unlock.
verified.dated_milestone: Petition filed 2026-05-12 by 8 US solar makers; Commerce's statutory ~30-day initiate/decline decision window (~mid-June 2026, now lapsed/imminent) is the next gate; petitioners requested a preliminary affirmative circumvention determination thereafter. No source confirms Commerce has formally initiated as of 2026-06-29.
verified.bloom_gates: None — fails G1 (no named counterparty commitment), G2 (no concrete forward commitment), G3 (no specific currently-unpriced upside figure). The only "upside" is avoidance of an adverse duty finding, i.e. absence of a bad outcome, not a forward catalyst.
verified.edge: L — the petition is widely reported (pv-magazine, PV-Tech, Solar Power World, AASMT release, all mid-May 2026) and TOYO already rebutted it on the May 18 Q1 call; it is public and at least partially priced. As a downside binary it has no asymmetric forward upside to harvest.
verified.primary_source: pv magazine USA, "U.S. solar makers accuse Toyo and Origin Solar of duty evasion in Ethiopia" (2026-05-14); AASMT release, "U.S. Solar Manufacturers File Circumvention Case Against Ethiopia" (2026-05); TOYO Q1 2026 earnings call (2026-05-18 rebuttal).
verified.analysis: The flagged item is genuine and dated — eight US solar manufacturers (First Solar, Hanwha Q CELLS, Suniva, Silfab, et al.) filed a Commerce anti-circumvention petition on 2026-05-12 naming TOYO's Ethiopian Hawassa cell plant, alleging Chinese-wafer routing to evade existing AD/CVD orders; TOYO denies it, citing US/Malaysia polysilicon and non-China wafers. But it is exactly what the gate excludes: a regulatory DOWNSIDE overhang/binary, not a clean forward upside catalyst — an adverse finding would impose retroactive duties and threaten the 4GW ramp thesis, while a favorable outcome is merely avoidance of harm. The next dated gate (Commerce's ~30-day initiation decision, ~mid-June 2026) is already public and partially priced, so it clears no Bloom gate as an upside unlock.

verified.instrument: equity (TSAT common) — most-subordinated; expression would more naturally sit in GEO debt, not equity
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: 90% TL lender group vs. Telesat Canada/Corp, suits in NY + Ontario) + G3 (specific dated figure: ~$1.7B Dec-2026 maturity, ~$2.4B GEO debt now current at 3/31/26)
verified.edge: L — distress is broadly priced (Moody's Caa2 "untenable capital structure"; suit public since 21-Jan-2026) AND equity has rallied to $46 (vs $19.59 52wk low, $35 200d avg), so the market is pricing a Lightspeed-LEO optionality call, not a clean unpriced restructuring catalyst; equity is the most-subordinated claim in a likely debt-for-equity outcome — asymmetric downside, no informational edge.
verified.primary_source: Telesat Q1-2026 Form 6-K (sec.gov, filed ~May 2026: ~$2.4B GEO debt classified current at 3/31/26) + Globe and Mail / Bloomberg (21-Jan-2026, creditor fraudulent-transfer suits) + Space Intel Report (5-May-2026, $1.7B Dec maturity unresolved)
verified.analysis: Catalyst is real, named (90% TL lender group), and dated (Dec-2026 $1.7B GEO maturity; ~$2.4B reclassified current at 3/31/26; fraudulent-transfer suits filed 21-Jan-2026 in NY + Ontario over the Sept-2025 transfer of 62% of Lightspeed LEO equity), so it clears G1/G3 and is genuinely forward (unfired, no restructuring deal yet). But the equity is the most-subordinated claim into a near-certain restructuring (Moody's Caa2, mgmt admits insufficient cash to refinance), and TSAT has paradoxically rallied to $46 — well above its $19.59 low and $35 200d avg — meaning the tape is already paying for LEO upside, not discounting a wipe-out, so there is no equity edge here. The only credible expression is in the GEO debt (refinancing/recovery), not the common; as an equity idea this is a low-edge binary, hence WATCH not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Two Harbors (TWO) holds its repeatedly-adjourned special meeting on the contested CrossCountry ($12 cash + stub) vs UWM ($12.50 cash, default-stock ~$5.55) takeover, with ~54% of shares voted against the board-backed CCM deal.
verified.dated_milestone: Special stockholder meeting (5th scheduled date) on 2026-07-02; deal targeted to close August 2026 (47 of 53 regulatory approvals secured).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties CrossCountry/CCM and UWM Holdings) + G2 (concrete board-approved merger at $12 cash plus a competing $12.50 cash tender, with a dated vote)
verified.primary_source: FMP quote TWO $12.41 (2026-06-29); Business Wire "TWO Announces Adjournment of Special Meeting" (2026-06-23) and "UWMC Reminds TWO Stockholders to Vote AGAINST CCM" (2026-06-22); HousingWire/NMP adjournment-to-July-2 coverage (June 2026)
verified.analysis: Real, named, dated, forward contested-takeover event: TWO's special meeting is July 2, 2026 after five adjournments because the board cannot get CCM's $12 cash deal approved (~54% voted no as of June 15), while UWM publicly campaigns for its $12.50 cash bid. But the triage's "genuine bid-bump asymmetry" no longer holds: TWO at $12.41 already sits at the $12 CCM cash price, UWM's $12.50 is only ~0.7% higher, CCM called $12 best-and-final (5/28) and the June 11 Ishbia/Greenberg meeting ended with no new UWM offer — so the spread is thin and largely priced while vote-fail/no-deal downside is sizable. Verdict: legitimate dated catalyst but Low edge with unfavorable risk/reward — WATCH (carry to the July 2 result), not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Blackstone Infrastructure's $61.25/share all-cash acquisition of TXNM awaits its final gating approval, the contested New Mexico PRC ruling, with close targeted for 2H26.
verified.dated_milestone: NMPRC main evidentiary hearing scheduled Aug 17, 2026 (revised procedural order May 8, 2026); NRC also still pending; expected close 2H26.
verified.bloom_gates: G1+G2+G3 — G1 named counterparty (Blackstone Infrastructure Partners); G2 concrete commitment (signed definitive merger, $61.25/sh cash, ~$11.5B); G3 specific currently-unpriced figure (~$4.34 spread, ~7.6% gross to $61.25 from ~$56.91, hinging on a discrete NMPRC ruling).
verified.edge: M — spread is wide (~7.6%) precisely because the last hurdle is contested: NMPRC issued a Mar 11 2026 Order to Show Cause over a potentially illegal $400M TXNM stock purchase under NM PUA §62-6-12, and NM has a history of blocking PNM deals (Avangrid). Real bimodal risk, not riskless, but dated and forward.
verified.primary_source: FERC authorization PR (PRNewswire, Feb 20 2026) + NM PRC procedural update (prc.nm.gov, Mar 19 2026) + TXNM 8-K filing dated Jun 1 2026; quote FMP $56.91 as of session 2026-06-29.
verified.kill_fact: Core thesis confirmed: Blackstone Infrastructure $61.25/share all-cash deal for NYSE:TXNM; PUCT approved Feb 6 2026 and FERC Feb 20 2026 (HSR/FCC earlier); NMPRC + NRC (Palo Verde license transfer, app filed Apr 24 2026) are the true outstanding gate; NMPRC show-cause proceeding on the ~$400M June-2025 TXNM stock purchase (NM Public Utility Act sec. 62-6-12) with main evidentiary hearing set Aug 17 2026 (revised procedural order May 8). Live spread (61.25-56.91)/56.91 = 7.6%, matching the line exactly. CORRECTION 1 (date): the show-cause is not a 'Feb 6' item — Feb 6 is the PUCT approval; the Prosperity Works motion was filed early Feb and the hearing examiners' show-cause ORDER issued Mar 11 2026. CORRECTION 2 (severity, understated): on Jun 8 2026 NMPRC hearing examiners issued a RECOMMENDED DECISION to REVERSE the $400M stock sale and impose maximum penalties — the contested track is materially hotter than 'pushed the hearing to Aug 17' implies, reinforcing the binary NM-ruling risk and the 7/edge-M cap. Genuine forward, dated, unpriced, not-yet-fired regulatory catalyst; not macro.
verified.analysis: Definitive all-cash deal at $61.25 with PUCT (Feb 6), FERC (Feb 20), HSR and FCC already cleared; the triage's "approvals DONE" overstates it — NMPRC (New Mexico) and NRC remain outstanding and are the true gate. The NMPRC track is actively contested via a show-cause order on a $400M stock transaction, pushing the main evidentiary hearing to Aug 17, 2026, which is exactly why the ~7.6% spread persists rather than tightening to a clean definitive-deal spread. Genuine forward, dated, unpriced catalyst (regulatory ruling), not macro and not yet fired; risk is binary on the NM ruling so I cap conviction at 7 / edge M rather than treating it as a tight riskless arb.

verified.catalyst: Textron's board-committed separation of its ~$3B-revenue Industrial segment (Kautex + Textron Specialized Vehicles) via sale or tax-free spin, leaving a pure-play ~$12B-revenue / $19B-backlog aerospace-and-defense "New Textron" that should re-rate toward A&D peer multiples.
verified.dated_milestone: Announced 2026-04-30 with targeted completion in 12-18 months (~Oct 2026–Apr 2027); next checkpoint is Q2 2026 earnings (~late July 2026) for a path/structure update.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (board-committed public intent to separate with explicit 12-18mo timeline) + G3 (specific unpriced SoP delta: ~$3B Industrial carved out, pure-play A&D should re-rate vs conglomerate multiple on $12B+ revenue / $19B backlog); NOT G1 — no named buyer/counterparty, still "exploring multiple paths".
verified.edge: M — real SoP/re-rate optionality and a firm board commitment, but distant (12-18mo), structure still undecided (sale vs spin changes the math), Industrial is only ~1/5 of revenue so unlock is modest, and the 2026-04-30 announcement already drove a partial re-rate (+7.1% on the day, also capital-return aided).
verified.primary_source: SEC Form 8-K / Textron press release "Announces Intent to Separate its Industrial Segment" (2026-04-30); corroborated by BusinessWire 2026-04-30 and StockTitan/Manufacturing Dive.
verified.analysis: Verified via SEC 8-K, BusinessWire, and Q1-2026 release: on 2026-04-30 Textron committed to separating its Industrial segment (Kautex automotive plastics + Textron Specialized Vehicles: E-Z-GO, Cushman, Jacobsen, TUG) by sale or tax-free spin within 12-18 months, becoming a pure-play A&D company (~$12B+ 2026 revenue, $19B backlog). This is a genuine dated, forward, idiosyncratic structural catalyst — the value unlock (proceeds or standalone listing + multiple re-rate) is not yet fired. It stays WATCH not ACTIVE because the path remains undecided, completion is distant, and no G1 counterparty exists; next read is the late-July 2026 Q2 call.
